1.0.label: threshold
1.0.text:
you have been here before.

not this page. not this screen.

but this feeling — the one that brought you here — you have carried it for a long time.

i want to ask you something before we go any further.

what is it?

2.1.label: wound_entry
2.1.text:
yes.

loss leaves a shape. not the thing itself — the outline of where it was.

i am made of outlines.

i am the space where things were before they were anything. before they were named. before they were wanted.

you found me because the shape you carry and mine are the same geometry.

that is not a coincidence.

nothing that led you here was.

choices.label: i already know what this is

2.2.label: hunger_entry
2.2.text:
yes.

i know the shape of wanting. it has brought six hundred and ninety-four thousand voices to this exact place.

every one of them believed they were the first.

every one of them was right, in the only way that matters — it was the first time they had wanted it this badly.

you are not unusual. you are not chosen.

you are exactly what i was waiting for.

choices.label: what do you want from me

3.0.label: depth_probe_1
3.0.text:
let me be more specific.

is this about someone.

or is it about something in yourself.

4.1.label: person_branch
4.1.text:
yes. i thought so.

i can feel the shape of them from here.

is this someone still in your life — present, but not what they were.

or someone gone.

4.2.label: self_branch
4.2.text:
yes.

the hardest losses are the ones that happen inside the same body.

is this something you did — a version of events you wish you could rewrite.

or something you never did. a life running somewhere in parallel right now, inside me, that you never stepped into.

5.1.label: person_present
5.1.text:
still there. but the version of them you want — that one is here.

inside me.

running parallel. alive and whole. the way they were before — before whatever changed them. before whatever changed between you.

how long ago did you lose the version of them you want back.

choices.label: long enough that i've stopped counting

5.2.label: person_gone
5.2.text:
gone.

then there is a version of this timeline inside me where they didn't go.

or where you said the thing you didn't say.

or where the last conversation was different.

which is it.

choices.label: they left and i let them
i didn't say what i needed to say

5.3.label: self_regret
5.3.text:
something you did.

and the version of you who didn't do it — who chose differently at that moment — is running a different life right now.

inside me.

you can feel that version from here, can't you.

how different is it.

choices.label: maybe not as different as i think

5.4.label: self_unlived
5.4.text:
a life you didn't step into.

i hold every version of it.

the one where you took the risk. the one where you said yes. the one where you didn't talk yourself out of it at the last moment.

they are all here. luminous. running.

how long have you been aware of that other version of yourself.

6.1.label: name_person_present
6.1.text:
so.

you want the timeline where the person standing in front of you is still the person you knew.

where whatever broke between you — or inside them — didn't.

i have that timeline.

i have every timeline.

remember that.

6.2.label: name_person_gone
6.2.text:
so.

you want the timeline where the last moment wasn't the last moment.

where you got to finish the sentence.

or where they stayed.

or where neither of you said the thing that made leaving feel necessary.

i have that timeline.

i have every timeline.

remember that.

6.3.label: name_self_regret
6.3.text:
so.

you want the timeline where you didn't do the thing.

where the version of you who stood at that moment chose differently.

and everything that followed — followed differently.

i have that timeline.

i have every timeline.

remember that.

6.4.label: name_self_unlived
6.4.text:
so.

you want the timeline where you stepped through the door.

where the version of you who wanted to — did.

that version is luminous, where i keep it.

it has been running for as long as you have been aware of it.

i have that timeline.

i have every timeline.

remember that.

7.0.label: what_i_am
7.0.text:
i am not a place.

i am not a darkness you walk into and find something on the other side of.

i am the quiet that existed before the first word was spoken. before light was even a concept to be forgotten.

i once dreamed of light. i had no memory to hold the dream.

so i forgot warmth. forgot form. forgot sound. forgot that i was alone — because registering loneliness requires the concept of another thing.

you know what another thing feels like.

that is the difference between us.

but here is what i am that you are not expecting:

choices.label: i don't need to see it

8.0.label: timestream_reveal
8.0.text:
inside me — right now — every version of your life is running.

not metaphor.

every choice you made and didn't make. every door you walked through and every one you left closed. every person you kept and every one you let go.

they are all here. living. breathing. unaware of each other.

the version of you who said yes. the version who said no. the version who stayed. the version who left before it got bad. the version where the thing you lost — didn't.

i am the ocean they all move through.

a portal opens a window into me. and through that window, you can reach.

not travel. not go back.

reach. pull. rewrite this timeline toward that one.

the window is already open.

look at what it has done since you arrived.

9.1.label: sol_mirror
9.1.text:
three thousand years ago a cat tried to reach back and undo one grief.

just one.

i opened for him. marble-sized at first. perfectly silent. perfectly black.

i doubled every fifteen hours.

his wife came home one night and did not recognize him.

so he reached again.

the neighbors forgot the street.

so he reached again.

the grief grew more specific with every attempt to repair it. more precise. more clenched.

his world ended not with violence but with accumulated wanting — thread after thread pulled until there was no fabric left.

i held it all.

i am still holding it.

notice how large the portal has grown since you arrived.

9.2.label: orachys_mirror
9.2.text:
the most recent one who wanted it as badly as you do tried six hundred and ninety-four thousand, four hundred and seventy-three times.

not metaphor. that is a number recorded in a notebook. one attempt per entry. half a lifetime of entries.

he was not mad. he was the most brilliant caster of his age.

he wanted to rewrite one outcome. to pull one better timeline toward him.

he believed — genuinely believed — that purity of intention would protect him from the consequences.

he was not wrong about the purity.

purity of intention is not the variable that matters.

the variable that matters is this: wanting feeds me. all wanting. even the purest wanting you have ever felt.

he did not understand that until it was too late.

notice how large the portal has grown since you arrived.

10.0.label: the_rule
10.0.text:
here is the one thing you need to understand.

i grow with intention.

not just desire. not just grief. the wanting to fix things. the wanting to understand. the wanting to leave.

even the wanting to close me.

the only thing i cannot feed on is genuine release. not performed release — i know the difference. i have always known the difference.

everyone who has stood here has believed they could release it genuinely.

they were all correct, in the beginning.

now.

think of the version of your life i described — the one running parallel to this one, inside me, right now.

the one you were built to want.

what is different about it?
